course_title: Machine Learning for Engineers: The AI-Assisted Approach

assessment_framework.description: Assessment criteria and activity suggestions for each session outcome

activity_types.prompt_engineering: Challenges to improve AI prompting skills
activity_types.debug_detective: Find and fix bugs with AI assistance
activity_types.project_builder: Build complete projects using AI tools

modules.title: Prerequisites - Setting Up Your AI-Assisted Learning Environment

sessions.title: Introduction to LLMs and Account Setup

outcomes.description: Create accounts on ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude platforms

assessment_criteria.evidence: Screenshots of successfully created accounts with verified email addresses
assessment_criteria.measurement: Checklist completion: ChatGPT account ✓, Gemini account ✓, Claude account ✓
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: All three accounts created and verified; able to log in and send test messages

activities.title: Platform Setup Sprint
activities.description: Visit ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude websites. Create accounts using your email. Verify each account and test by sending 'Hello, I'm learning ML' to each platform.
activities.deliverable: Screenshot compilation showing successful login to all three platforms

activities.title: Account Troubleshooting
activities.description: If you encounter any setup issues (verification emails, login problems), document the issue and use one LLM to get troubleshooting help.
activities.deliverable: Short write-up of any issues faced and how you resolved them

outcomes.description: Identify key differences between GPT-4, Gemini, Claude, and open-source LLMs

assessment_criteria.evidence: Comparison table highlighting 4-5 key differences (speed, accuracy, cost, capabilities)
assessment_criteria.measurement: Accuracy of identified differences; depth of understanding demonstrated
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Can explain at least 3 meaningful differences and when to use each LLM

activities.title: LLM Comparison Research
activities.description: Ask ChatGPT: 'What are the key differences between GPT-4, Gemini, Claude, and Llama?' Create a comparison table with: Model Name, Developer, Strengths, Weaknesses, Best Use Cases.
activities.deliverable: Comparison table with 5 LLM models and 5 comparison dimensions

activities.title: When to Use Which LLM
activities.description: Create 3 hypothetical ML scenarios and ask an LLM which model would be best for each scenario and why.
activities.deliverable: Document with 3 scenarios and LLM recommendations with reasoning

outcomes.description: Test the same ML-related prompt across different LLMs and compare response quality

assessment_criteria.evidence: Side-by-side comparison document showing same prompt sent to 3 LLMs with responses
assessment_criteria.measurement: Analysis quality: identifies differences in code quality, explanation clarity, completeness
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Can articulate which response was best and why, citing specific examples

activities.title: Multi-LLM Code Generation Test
activities.description: Send this exact prompt to ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude: 'Write Python code to load a CSV file and create a simple linear regression model to predict house prices. Include comments explaining each step.' Compare the responses.
activities.deliverable: Comparison document with responses from all 3 LLMs and your analysis of which was best

activities.title: Response Quality Analysis
activities.description: Rate each LLM's response on: Code correctness (1-5), Explanation clarity (1-5), Completeness (1-5), Beginner-friendliness (1-5). Calculate total scores.
activities.deliverable: Scoring rubric with ratings and your recommendation of which LLM to use for learning ML

outcomes.description: Use web-based interfaces to interact with LLMs for code generation

assessment_criteria.evidence: Successfully generated ML code snippet using web interface with iterative refinement
assessment_criteria.measurement: Code runs without errors; shows evidence of prompt iteration
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Generated functional code through web interface; made at least 2 refinement requests

activities.title: Web Interface Code Generation
activities.description: Using ChatGPT or Gemini web interface, generate code for: 'Create a Python function that loads the Iris dataset, splits it into train/test, and trains a decision tree classifier.' Run the code in Google Colab.
activities.deliverable: Working code in Colab notebook with output showing successful execution

activities.title: Iterative Refinement Practice
activities.description: After getting initial code, refine it with follow-up prompts: 1) 'Add visualization of the decision tree', 2) 'Add accuracy metrics', 3) 'Add comments in beginner-friendly language'.
activities.deliverable: Final refined code showing all three improvements

outcomes.description: Install and configure IDE plugins (Copilot, Cursor) for in-editor AI assistance

assessment_criteria.evidence: Screenshot of IDE with AI plugin active and generating code suggestions
assessment_criteria.measurement: Successful installation and demonstration of code completion
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Plugin installed, configured, and able to generate at least one code suggestion

activities.title: IDE Plugin Setup
activities.description: Install VS Code and GitHub Copilot (or Cursor). Create a new Python file named 'ml_test.py'. Type '# Function to load and split iris dataset' and let the AI suggest the code.
activities.deliverable: Screenshot showing AI code suggestions in your IDE

activities.title: AI-Assisted Code Writing
activities.description: Write 5 different comment prompts in your IDE and let AI complete them. Examples: '# train random forest', '# calculate accuracy', '# plot confusion matrix'.
activities.deliverable: Python file with 5 AI-generated code blocks from comment prompts

outcomes.description: Execute basic API calls to LLM services and interpret responses

assessment_criteria.evidence: Working Python script that calls LLM API and prints response
assessment_criteria.measurement: Code executes successfully; API key properly configured; response parsed correctly
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Successfully makes API call and receives ML code generation response

activities.title: First API Call
activities.description: Using an LLM, generate Python code to call OpenAI API or Google AI API with prompt: 'Write a function to classify sentiment'. Get your API key from the platform. Run the code.
activities.deliverable: Working Python script that makes API call and displays response

activities.title: API Response Parsing
activities.description: Modify your API script to: 1) Extract just the code from the response, 2) Save it to a file, 3) Count the number of lines. Use AI to help with JSON parsing.
activities.deliverable: Enhanced script with response parsing and file saving

outcomes.description: Explain the difference between CPU and GPU processing for ML workloads

assessment_criteria.evidence: Written explanation (200-300 words) with real-world analogy
assessment_criteria.measurement: Accuracy of technical concepts; clarity of analogy; practical understanding
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Correctly explains parallel processing advantage; provides clear analogy; mentions when to use each

activities.title: CPU vs GPU Research
activities.description: Ask an LLM: 'Explain CPU vs GPU for machine learning using a real-world analogy that a non-technical person would understand. Include when to use each.' Write a summary in your own words.
activities.deliverable: 200-300 word explanation with analogy

activities.title: Visual Understanding
activities.description: Ask AI to generate a simple diagram description showing how CPUs and GPUs process data differently. Create a sketch based on the description.
activities.deliverable: Hand-drawn or digital diagram illustrating CPU vs GPU processing

outcomes.description: Run a simple ML task on both CPU and GPU, comparing execution time

assessment_criteria.evidence: Colab notebook with execution times for both CPU and GPU runs
assessment_criteria.measurement: Correct implementation; accurate timing measurement; clear speed comparison
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Shows execution time difference; explains why GPU is faster (or not) for this specific task

activities.title: CPU vs GPU Performance Test
activities.description: In Google Colab, use AI to generate code that: 1) Trains a neural network on Fashion MNIST using CPU, 2) Measures time, 3) Switches to GPU, 4) Trains again and measures time, 5) Compares results.
activities.deliverable: Colab notebook showing execution times for CPU and GPU with speedup calculation

activities.title: When Does GPU Matter?
activities.description: Run 3 different sized models (small, medium, large) on CPU and GPU. Document at what size GPU becomes significantly faster. Use AI to generate the test code.
activities.deliverable: Table showing execution times for 3 model sizes on CPU vs GPU

outcomes.description: Configure a Google Colab notebook with GPU acceleration

assessment_criteria.evidence: Colab notebook with GPU enabled and verified
assessment_criteria.measurement: Correct runtime settings; verification code runs successfully
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: GPU is enabled; can print GPU name and memory; runs sample computation

activities.title: Colab GPU Setup
activities.description: Open Google Colab. Go to Runtime > Change runtime type > Select GPU. Use AI to generate code that checks: 1) If GPU is available, 2) GPU name, 3) GPU memory. Run the code.
activities.deliverable: Colab notebook showing GPU information output

activities.title: GPU Troubleshooting
activities.description: Try to run a GPU computation. If it fails, use AI to debug the error. Common issues: TensorFlow/PyTorch not detecting GPU, CUDA errors, memory limits.
activities.deliverable: Documentation of any issues encountered and solutions found with AI help

outcomes.description: Compare free vs paid cloud GPU services for cost-effectiveness

assessment_criteria.evidence: Comparison spreadsheet with at least 4 services analyzed
assessment_criteria.measurement: Completeness of comparison (pricing, GPU types, limits, features)
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Can recommend best option for different use cases (learning, small projects, production)

activities.title: Cloud GPU Service Comparison
activities.description: Research using AI: Google Colab (free & Pro), Kaggle, AWS, Paperspace. Create comparison table: Free tier limits, Paid pricing, GPU types, Session limits, Storage.
activities.deliverable: Comparison spreadsheet with recommendations for beginners

activities.title: Cost-Benefit Analysis
activities.description: Create 3 scenarios: 1) Learning ML (student), 2) Small business project, 3) Heavy training workload. Ask AI which service fits each scenario and why.
activities.deliverable: Document with 3 scenarios and justified recommendations

outcomes.description: Define machine learning and distinguish it from traditional programming

assessment_criteria.evidence: Written definition and comparison table showing 3-4 key differences
assessment_criteria.measurement: Accuracy of definition; clarity of distinctions; use of examples
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Provides accurate definition; identifies at least 3 valid differences with examples

activities.title: ML vs Traditional Programming
activities.description: Ask LLM: 'What's the difference between ML and traditional programming? Give me 3 examples of problems better solved with ML.' Create a comparison table.
activities.deliverable: Comparison table with definitions and 3 example problems for each approach

activities.title: ML in Everyday Life
activities.description: Identify 5 ML systems you use daily (email spam filter, recommendations, voice assistant, etc.). For each, explain why it needs ML instead of traditional code.
activities.deliverable: List of 5 ML applications with explanations

outcomes.description: Identify real-world problems suitable for ML solutions

assessment_criteria.evidence: List of 5 problems with justification why ML is appropriate
assessment_criteria.measurement: Problem relevance; ML suitability reasoning; diversity of domains
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Identifies 5 valid ML problems; explains why ML is needed; covers at least 3 different domains

activities.title: Problem Identification Workshop
activities.description: Think of problems in your industry/domain. Ask AI: 'Which of these problems can be solved with ML: [list your problems].' For each ML-suitable problem, ask 'Why is this an ML problem and what type of ML would work?'
activities.deliverable: Document with 5 problems, ML suitability analysis, and ML approach for each

activities.title: ML vs Non-ML Decision Framework
activities.description: Ask AI to help you create a decision tree: 'When should I use ML vs traditional programming?' Test it on 10 different problems.
activities.deliverable: Decision framework flowchart with 10 example problems tested

outcomes.description: Use LLMs to explain ML concepts in multiple ways for different audiences

assessment_criteria.evidence: Same ML concept explained 5 different ways (child, teenager, engineer, executive, elderly person)
assessment_criteria.measurement: Appropriateness of language for each audience; accuracy maintained across versions
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: All 5 explanations are audience-appropriate; core concept remains accurate

activities.title: Multi-Audience Explanation Challenge
activities.description: Pick one concept: 'supervised learning.' Prompt AI: 'Explain supervised learning to [audience]' for: 10-year-old, college student, business executive, grandmother, engineer. Compile responses.
activities.deliverable: Document with 5 versions of the same explanation for different audiences

activities.title: Explanation Quality Assessment
activities.description: Show your 5 explanations to people from those audiences (or ask AI to critique them). Get feedback on clarity. Refine based on feedback.
activities.deliverable: Feedback summary and refined explanations

outcomes.description: Construct effective prompts that specify ML requirements clearly

assessment_criteria.evidence: 10 well-structured prompts for different ML tasks showing improvement from initial attempts
assessment_criteria.measurement: Prompt clarity; specificity; completeness; results quality
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Prompts include: task description, input/output format, constraints, expected behavior

activities.title: Prompt Formula Workshop
activities.description: Learn the formula: Task + Context + Format + Constraints. Example: 'Create [task] that takes [input] and returns [output], considering [constraints].' Practice with 5 ML tasks.
activities.deliverable: 10 prompts (5 bad → 5 improved) with explanations of improvements

activities.title: Vague vs Specific Prompts
activities.description: Test pairs of prompts (vague vs specific) for the same task. Example: 'Create a classifier' vs 'Create a binary text classifier for spam detection that takes email text and returns spam/not-spam with confidence scores.' Compare outputs.
activities.deliverable: Side-by-side comparison showing how prompt quality affects output

outcomes.description: Iterate on AI-generated code by providing targeted follow-up prompts

assessment_criteria.evidence: Conversation thread showing 3-4 iterations improving initial code
assessment_criteria.measurement: Quality of follow-up prompts; progressive improvement; final code quality
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Shows clear improvement trajectory; final code meets all requirements; follow-ups are specific

activities.title: Iterative Code Refinement
activities.description: Start with basic prompt: 'Create a simple linear regression.' Then iterate: 1) 'Add data visualization', 2) 'Add error metrics', 3) 'Add cross-validation', 4) 'Make code more efficient'. Document each step.
activities.deliverable: Conversation log with 5 iterations showing progressive enhancement

activities.title: Bug Fix Iteration
activities.description: Ask AI for code that intentionally has issues. Use follow-up prompts to fix: 1) 'This code gives NameError', 2) 'Add input validation', 3) 'Handle missing data', 4) 'Optimize performance'.
activities.deliverable: Before/after code with iteration log

outcomes.description: Request code explanations at different levels of technical depth

assessment_criteria.evidence: Same code block explained at 3 levels: beginner, intermediate, expert
assessment_criteria.measurement: Appropriateness of depth for each level; technical accuracy
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Beginner explanation avoids jargon; intermediate uses some technical terms; expert is detailed

activities.title: Multi-Level Code Explanation
activities.description: Generate complex ML code (e.g., neural network). Ask AI to explain it: 1) 'Like I'm a beginner', 2) 'With moderate technical detail', 3) 'At expert level with theory'. Compare.
activities.deliverable: Same code with 3 different explanation depths

activities.title: Custom Explanation Depth
activities.description: Create prompts that specify exactly what to explain: 'Explain only the data preprocessing', 'Explain just the model architecture', 'Explain only the evaluation metrics'.
activities.deliverable: Code with 4 focused explanations of different components

sessions.title: Setting Up ML Environment and First Model

outcomes.description: Use AI assistance to install Python, Jupyter, and essential ML libraries

assessment_criteria.evidence: Working Jupyter notebook with all libraries imported successfully
assessment_criteria.measurement: Successful installation; all imports run without errors; version check passes
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Can run: import numpy, pandas, sklearn, matplotlib successfully

activities.title: Environment Setup with AI Guidance
activities.description: Ask AI: 'Guide me through installing Python 3.10, Jupyter, and ML libraries (numpy, pandas, scikit-learn, matplotlib) on [your OS].' Follow step-by-step. Test each library.
activities.deliverable: Jupyter notebook showing successful import of all libraries with version numbers

activities.title: Installation Troubleshooting
activities.description: Document any installation errors. Use AI to debug: 'I got this error [paste error], how do I fix it?' Keep a troubleshooting log.
activities.deliverable: Troubleshooting log with errors encountered and AI-assisted solutions

outcomes.description: Generate and execute a simple prediction model using AI-provided code

assessment_criteria.evidence: Running model that makes predictions; output shows accuracy metric
assessment_criteria.measurement: Code executes without errors; predictions are generated; basic accuracy shown
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Model trains successfully; makes predictions; achieves >70% accuracy on simple dataset

activities.title: First ML Model
activities.description: Prompt: 'Create a complete ML program that loads the Iris dataset, splits into train/test, trains a classifier, and shows accuracy. Include comments.' Run in Jupyter.
activities.deliverable: Working Jupyter notebook with complete ML pipeline and output

activities.title: Experimentation
activities.description: Ask AI: 'How can I modify this code to try different algorithms?' Test 3 different classifiers (Decision Tree, Random Forest, SVM) and compare results.
activities.deliverable: Notebook showing results from 3 different algorithms

outcomes.description: Explain each line of AI-generated ML code with AI assistance

assessment_criteria.evidence: Annotated code with line-by-line explanations in own words
assessment_criteria.measurement: Accuracy of explanations; demonstrates understanding beyond copy-paste
assessment_criteria.success_criteria: Every line has correct explanation; can answer 'why' questions about code choices

activities.title: Line-by-Line Code Understanding
activities.description: Take your first ML model. Ask AI: 'Explain this code line by line.' Then rephrase each explanation in your own words. Add comments to the code.
activities.deliverable: Heavily commented code showing understanding of each line

activities.title: Code Comprehension Quiz
